commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh My heritage is unto me like a speckled bird of prey, the birds round about are against her; come you, assemble all the beasts of the field, come to devour. Septuagint, "a den of the hyena: "Alexandrian, "robbers. "Grabe inserts, "or a den around it. "(Haydock) They have read áuss, "a den "instead of hait, "a bird. "Bo chart, (iii. ) however, shows that the latter word denotes various wild beasts, and nearly follows the Septuagint. Though the inheritance be most charming, it shall not pass unpunished.
